hypothesis: Systematic differences in GFN2-xTB relaxation energy between flow-matching and diffusion-based generative models arise primarily from inadequate sampling of torsional degrees of freedom rather than local bond geometry errors, such that increasing the number of inference steps in flow-matching models beyond 100 will selectively reduce torsion angle deviations and close the performance gap with diffusion-based models without proportionally improving bond length or bond angle accuracy.
